notes: This file is normative for the v0.56 shader-kernel execution model.
MPAR and MSEQ are the canonical shader-kernel block types in v0.56.
Contradictory legacy body-execution fragments are not promoted into v0.56 semantics.

kernel_body.termination_rule: kernel completion occurs when control reaches a legal body terminator along the taken in-body control-flow path

containment.external_entry: architectural control flow into the middle of a kernel is illegal and must raise E_BLOCK(EC_CFI)
containment.escaping_target: in-body control flow that resolves outside the current B.TEXT body region faults as E_BLOCK(EC_BFETCH)
containment.resume_target: kernel completion resumes at the header continuation point

exec_mask.name: p
exec_mask.read_write: architectural source and destination inside kernel bodies
exec_mask.vector_predication: vector instructions are implicitly predicated by p
exec_mask.inactive_lane_rule: inactive lanes follow the selected architectural merge/zero policy

mask_generation.inactive_lane_result: inactive lanes clear their destination bit to 0

instruction_domain.derivation_rule: if any operand names vt/vu/vm/vn the instruction executes as v.*; otherwise it executes as l.*
instruction_domain.broadcast_rule: scalar/group-domain source operands broadcast to all active lanes when used by v.*
instruction_domain.cross_domain_write_rule: v.* may not write scalar/group-domain destinations except through explicit reduction or mask-producing instructions

scalar_imports.binding_rule: allocated from repeated B.IOR descriptors in header order, then RegSrc0/RegSrc1/RegSrc2 within each descriptor

bridged_memory.ordering_domain: shared TSO domain with BCC and TMA/MTC operations
bridged_memory.kernel_policy: normal BCC scalar memory issue is closed while a shader kernel is active

local_memory.bounds_rule: access must remain within the tracked size of the bound tile base
